mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which typically precede corporate earnings declines and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real estate financing (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) raises borrowing costs for corporations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during active tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 110) signals weakening manufacturing activity, which typically precedes earnings declines and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 100k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) indicates a strong labor market, which drives corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in strong-labor regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 1500) during periods of rising real estate financing (RSAFS > 350k) indicate a housing boom, which drives consumer confidence and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in housing boom regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2%) signals accommodative monetary policy, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
